paper_title: Calcareous Nannofossil Analysis and Study Area Description

methods.description: This method involves the preparation and examination of sediment samples to analyze calcareous nannofossils. The analysis is used to obtain accurate biostratigraphic estimations and to correlate with regional and global stratigraphy and time scales for the Miocene marine successions.

steps.description: Preparation of samples followed the standard smear slide technique described by Perch Nielsen (1985).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare sediment samples using smear slide technique

steps.description: All sediment samples were routinely examined under a light microscope at a magnification of 1250 x, carried out using a Leica DMSP polarising light microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: examine samples under light microscope at 1250x magnification

steps.description: Up to 100 fields of view have been investigated per slide to obtain accurate biostratigraphic estimations.
steps.searchable_summary: investigate up to 100 fields of view per slide

steps.description: Calcareous nannofossil biostratigraphy, mainly based on the appearance/disappearance and relative abundance patterns of selected species, provided high time resolution and accurate correlation with regional and global stratigraphy and time scales.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze nannofossil biostratigraphy for time resolution and correlation

steps.description: Record abundances of the taxa encountered as A, C, R, P, RW based on specimen frequency per field of view.
steps.searchable_summary: record taxa abundances based on specimen frequency
